Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper identifies OsABCC1 as an ABC transporter for vacuolar As compartmentation; title/keywords and text support ABCC-type ATP-binding cassette transporter.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Rice is given as Oryza sativa L.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: The Dongjin cultivar was used for transformation.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Results show RCc3-driven OsABCC1-containing lines reduced brown-rice As; RCc3p-AEY had strong root retention, reduced xylem sap/shoot/grain As, and ubiquitous expression constructs were ineffective. The specific '~70%' reduction matches '30% of WT As levels' in brown rice.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: These methods are all described in Results/Methods: transgenic overexpression, promoter comparison, sucrose gradient/immunoblot localization, qRT-PCR, As profiling in grain/tissues/xylem sap, and stem As feeding assay.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: Figure 1c and text state OsABCC1-V5 was targeted to the vacuolar membrane.

Applied_Promoters.reason: OsABCC1 was expressed under RCc3 and ZmUBI promoters in different constructs.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: RCc3 promoter activity was shown in root cortical cells/endodermis and phloem cells of nodes/internodes; this supports engineered expression pattern.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: The text says endogenous OsABCC1 is 'likely already expressed' in node tissues, but this was not directly measured in this paper.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: The paper coexpresses OsABCC1 with ScYCF1, but does not demonstrate a physical or direct molecular interaction between the proteins.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: All listed treatments/conditions appear: As(III), DMA, normal soil basal As, and grain-filling stage feeding assays.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: This effect is directly supported by reduced root-to-shoot and shoot-to-grain As translocation and proposed vacuolar sequestration in root cortex/internode phloem tissues.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The measured terminal outcome is arsenic accumulation in brown rice.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Arsenic is treated as a metalloid contaminant in the study context.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The summary/conclusion explicitly links lower grain As to improved human health and reduced toxicity/cancer risk.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Brown rice grain was the main endpoint measured.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper concludes this technology could reduce As intake and emphasizes tissue-specific expression; future improvement with extrusion traits for high-As soils is discussed.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Conclusion states high vacuolar sequestration in nongrain tissues could lead to hyperaccumulation and cell damage under excess As, while Table 1 shows no major agronomic penalty under tested conditions.

Other_Important_Info.reason: DMA was not recognized as a major substrate, favoring inorganic As(III), and Cd accumulation/translocation was unaffected.

corrections.reason: The paper coexpresses OsABCC1 with ScYCF1, but does not demonstrate a physical or direct molecular interaction between the proteins.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: ScYCF1/YCF1 is presented as a vacuolar As sequestration gene/ABC transporter; YCF1 is an ABCC-type transporter in this context.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The gene name ScYCF1 denotes Saccharomyces cerevisiae YCF1; yeast assays refer to ycf1 null mutant. This is reasonably supported though the full Latin binomial is not printed verbatim in the provided text.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: The paper states codon-optimized ScYCF1 was introduced into rice, and in combination with OsABCC1 and c-ECS under RCc3 it reduced As translocation to shoots/grains; yeast and rice DMA tests indicated YCF1/ScYCF1 does not recognize DMA as a major substrate.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: These methods are reported: transgenic expression, promoter comparison, tissue/grain As profiling, yeast DMA sensitivity assay, and stem feeding assay.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: ScYCF1 is introduced as a vacuolar As sequestration gene, but this paper directly localized OsABCC1-V5, not ScYCF1 protein in rice.

Applied_Promoters.reason: ScYCF1 was expressed using RCc3 and ZmUBI promoters in different constructs.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: RCc3 promoter activity in root cortex and phloem-associated node/internode tissues supports this engineered expression pattern for ScYCF1-containing constructs.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Coexpression with OsABCC1 does not establish direct protein interaction.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: As(III), DMA, normal soil basal As, and grain-filling As feeding were all used.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Within stacked RCc3p-AEY constructs, ScYCF1 contributes to enhanced vacuolar As sequestration and reduced grain As, but not as an isolated single-gene test.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Arsenic in brown rice is the measured terminal outcome.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Reduced grain As is explicitly tied to improved food safety/human health.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The study supports use of ScYCF1 in combination with OsABCC1 and c-ECS, and shows tissue-specific expression is critical.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The general transgenic strategy may cause As hyperaccumulation/cell damage under excess As, with no agronomic penalty under tested conditions.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper explicitly states codon-optimized ScYCF1/YCF1 was synthesized and results support that it is not a DMA transporter.

corrections.reason: Coexpression with OsABCC1 does not establish direct protein interaction.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper explicitly calls Lsi2 a major As efflux transporter; silicon/arsenite transporter identity is consistent with cited background.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Dongjin is the rice cultivar used in the study, though Lsi2 itself was not manipulated.

Source_Species.reason: Lsi2 discussed is the rice transporter.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The source species is Oryza sativa in context.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: This accurately reflects that Lsi2 is described as major As efflux transporter loading As into xylem, that OsABCC1/ScYCF1 may inhibit radial movement toward Lsi2, and that Lsi2 was not experimentally manipulated.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: The statement correctly says it was background inference only and not directly tested.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: Figure 4/conclusion state Lsi2 is localized to the proximal side of the endodermal layer, i.e., plasma membrane domain.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: The paper places Lsi2 in the endodermis.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: The paper does not state that Lsi2 physically or directly interacts with Lsi1.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Lsi2 is described as mediating As loading into xylem as background mechanism.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The paper discusses As translocation to shoot/grain, but 'terminal metabolite' is not a gene-specific measured endpoint for Lsi2 because it was not tested here.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Xylem/shoot/grain reflects the proposed transport route, but Lsi2 was not directly assayed in this study.

Other_Important_Info.reason: It is indeed a background gene included in the model/discussion and not a core experimental gene.

corrections.reason: The paper does not state that Lsi2 physically or directly interacts with Lsi1.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper only mentions Lsi1 in Figure 4 as a transporter for uptake/translocation; 'aquaporin-like influx transporter' is background knowledge not explicitly stated in the text provided.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Dongjin is the cultivar used, though Lsi1 itself was not studied experimentally.

Source_Species.reason: Lsi1 discussed is the rice transporter in the model.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: This accurately states Lsi1 is only mentioned in the model and not functionally tested in Results.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: It was only mentioned as background mechanistic context.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: Figure 4 places Lsi1 in root exodermis/endodermis but does not explicitly say 'plasma membrane' in the text body, though the model implies membrane localization.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Figure 4/conclusion place Lsi1 in root exodermis and endodermis context.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: No direct interaction with Lsi2 is stated or tested.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: The model describes Lsi1 as contributing to As uptake/translocation in WT plants.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Arsenic translocation is a process, not a directly measured terminal product for Lsi1 in this paper.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Root-to-shoot transport stream is inferred from the model, not directly measured for Lsi1.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Lsi1 is indeed only a background gene and not a core experimental subject.

corrections.reason: No direct interaction with Lsi2 is stated or tested.

Enzyme_Name.reason: The paper identifies c-ECS as bacterial γ-glutamylcysteine synthetase.

EC_Number.reason: No EC number is given in the paper text.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: It is described as a key enzyme in glutathione synthesis.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: As γ-glutamylcysteine synthetase, it catalyzes the γ-glutamylcysteine formation step; this is directly inferable from the enzyme name.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper explicitly calls c-ECS 'a key enzyme in glutathione synthesis'.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: It acts upstream of glutathione/phytochelatin accumulation; this is directly inferable from the described role.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The paper says c-ECS was used to increase nonprotein thiols (glutathione and phytochelatin) concentration, but does not directly quantify either metabolite individually.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Glutathione and phytochelatins are nonprotein thiol metabolites, as described.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper links thiols to As chelation/colocalization and vacuolar sequestration contributing to reduced grain As.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: The paper mentions increasing nonprotein thiols in the cytosol and shows high thiol accumulation in vacuoles of root cortex cells in RCc3p-AEY plants, but does not specifically assign glutathione/phytochelatin to 'root cortex vacuoles/cytosolic thiol pool' as a precise field value.

Source_Species.reason: The paper states bacterial c-ECS and later specifies Escherichia coli.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The source species is given as Escherichia coli.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: The wild-type strain K12 is explicitly stated.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: The paper states c-ECS was coexpressed to increase nonprotein thiols; high thiol accumulation in root cortex cells accompanied reduced As translocation in RCc3p-AEY; and c-ECS was used supportively rather than isolated as a sole determinant.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Transgenic coexpression, qRT-PCR, mBB thiol staining, and As profiling in roots/shoots/grains were performed.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: The paper does not explicitly state c-ECS protein subcellular localization as cytosolic.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The study supports c-ECS as a stacking component with vacuolar As transporters; the claim that it is likely insufficient alone is reasonable because AE/AEY were effective whereas rationale emphasizes combination, but c-ECS alone was not tested.

Applied_Promoters.reason: c-ECS was driven by ZmUBI in RCc3p-AE/AEY and by OsActin2 in UBIp-AE/AEY.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: c-ECS was constitutively expressed under ubiquitous promoters in engineered rice.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper names the enzyme but does not state 'Glutamate-cysteine ligase family'.

Primary_Substrate.reason: The substrates L-glutamate, L-cysteine, and ATP are not stated in the paper.

Primary_Product.reason: As γ-glutamylcysteine synthetase, production of γ-glutamylcysteine is directly inferable from the enzyme name.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: This biochemical reaction description is accurate background knowledge but not explicitly stated in the paper.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: The paper supports that c-ECS supplies precursor/thiol pool for glutathione and phytochelatin biosynthesis.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: The paper supports increased thiol accumulation and contribution to reduced As translocation when stacked with OsABCC1 and ScYCF1, but not as a standalone gene effect.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: As(III) hydroponic exposure, normal soil basal As, and grain-filling As feeding are described.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The paper discusses high As sequestration in nongrain tissues causing possible damage under excess As and shows no agronomic penalty under tested field conditions.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: No direct protein interactions with OsABCC1 or ScYCF1 are shown; these genes were only coexpressed.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper explicitly states this was a bacterial gene used for metabolic engineering and its effect was demonstrated only in stacked constructs, not as a standalone tested gene.

corrections.reason: No EC number is given in the paper text.
The paper does not explicitly state c-ECS protein subcellular localization as cytosolic.
The substrates L-glutamate, L-cysteine, and ATP are not stated in the paper.
No direct protein interactions with OsABCC1 or ScYCF1 are shown; these genes were only coexpressed.
